etymology.description: semantic 山(山) (shān, "mountain") + phonetic 网(網) (wǎng) -> ridge
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 山(山) (shān, "mountain") as the semantic element, indicating the connection to landscapes, specifically ridges, with 网(網) (wǎng) providing the phonetic cue. The use of 山(山) logically aligns with the meaning related to mountainous features like ridges.
etymology.simplification: 岡(岡) was simplified to 冈(岡) by removing the 山(山) component and simplifying 网(網) (wǎng) to form a visually simpler character.

examples.chinese: 他登上了山冈(他登上了山岡)。
小径穿过草冈(小徑穿過草岡)。
examples.english: The path crosses the grassy ridge.

examples.chinese: 日出时(日出時)，光芒洒在冈顶(光芒灑在岡頂)。
examples.pinyin: Rìchū shí, guāngmáng sǎ zài gāng dǐng.
examples.english: At sunrise, the light bathes the ridge top.
